ORDER ON DEFENDANT ATHENA ZEIGLER'S DEMURRER TO THE COMPLAINT

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT ATHENA ZEIGLER'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT. BASED ON REVIEW OF THE PAPERS FILED BY THE PARTIES, THIS CIVIL CASE IS STAYED PER CCP 128(A)(8) PENDING THE CONCLUSION OF ALL PROCEEDINGS IN THE FAMILY COURT RELATING TO THE CLAIMS ALLEGED BY PLAINTIFF NICHOLAS MEYERS. MR. MEYERS' CLAIMS REGARDING THE 401K ACCOUNT WERE PLAINLY PART OF THE FAMILY COURT ACTION (FDI-17-787579) (SEE MINI-MINUTES FOR DETAILS). ORDER IS SIGNED. JUDGE HAROLD KAHN; CLERK: WILLIAM TRUPEK; NOT REPORTED. (302/HEK)

DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Dec-13-2018 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Nathaniel Bigger, Esq., Law Offices of Nathaniel Bigger, Telephone 415-295-4735. appearing for Defendant Athena Zeigler;
No other appearance.

The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT ATHENA ZEIGLER's DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT

Based on review of the papers filed by the parties, this civil case is stayed per CCP 128(a)(8) pending the conclusion of all proceedings in the family court relating to the claims alleged by plaintiff Nicholas Meyers. Mr. Meyers' claims regarding the 401K account were plainly part of the family court action (FDI-17-787579). (Marital Settlement Agreement ("MSA"),VI.B(b); Qualified Domestic Relations Order, 2:11; 2:20-21.) Mr. Meyers must return to family court for clarification of any issues pertaining to the 401K account. The family court expressly reserved its jurisdiction to resolve such matters per section X of the MSA. This civil court cannot undermine the family court's prior adjudications. "After a family law court acquires jurisdiction to divide community property in a dissolution action, no other department of a superior court may make an order adversely affecting that division." (Askew v. Askew (1994) 22 Cal.App.4th 942, 961; see also Burkle v. Burkle (2006) 144 Cal.App.4th 387, 393.) The record is not clear whether the family court made any explicit or implicit rulings with regard to the parties' dog Luna. Since California considers a dog personal property and the family court resolved the parties' personal property issues, this court believes that the family court should resolve and/or clarify that issue too. (Civil Code 3340; Plotnik v. Meihaus (2012) 208 Cal.App.4th 1590, 1606.) In an abundance of caution, this court will stay this action rather than dismiss it outright to allow Mr. Meyers the opportunity to return to the family court for guidance. If Mr. Meyers fails to seek relief with the family court by February 28, 2019, defendant Athena Ziegler may apply to this court ex parte for a dismissal of this action. Order is signed.

Judge Harold Kahn; Clerk: William Trupek; Not reported. (302/HEK)

DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
